title: aira cespitosa var. major
intro: Aira cespitosa var. major, also known as the greater hair grass, is a variety of grass native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Aira cespitosa var. major can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, which has been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory infections.
